title: cineraria palustris subsp. congesta
intro: Cineraria palustris subsp. congesta, also known as the marsh cineraria or marsh daisy, is a rare, subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, due to its health benefits. Cineraria palustris subsp. congesta can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
